ontology.foodon_gap_note: FoodOn has a general hamburger class (FOODON_00002764); no specific triple-patty cheeseburger subclass exists. Closest defensible match used.
ontology.foodex2_gap_note: FoodEx2 code A00LC covers hamburger/cheeseburger as a composite meat-bread dish; no triple-patty variant code exists. Closest defensible code used.
ontology.snomed_ct_gap_note: No specific SNOMED CT concept for triple cheeseburger identified in published SNOMED CT release.

serving.natural_language_portion: One triple cheeseburger weighing approximately 270 g, comprising three beef patties, three slices of processed cheese, a sesame-seed bun, pickles, onions, ketchup, and mustard.

nutrient_source.title: Triple Cheeseburger — Singapore Health Promotion Board Foodo Database
nutrient_source.source_match_notes: Input record carries HPB provenance with confidence 0.95 and review_status reviewed. All core per-100g values are taken directly from the HPB Foodo entry for Triple Cheeseburger (cr_id G00010121). Saturated fat is estimated from USDA FoodData Central SR Legacy entry 21107 (Cheeseburger, double patty, with condiments and vegetables) as HPB does not separately report saturated fat for this item; the USDA value of 4.5 g/100g is used as a conservative close analogue and documented accordingly.

gi_source.title: International tables of glycemic index and glycemic load values: 2008 — Atkinson FS, Foster-Powell K, Brand-Miller JC
gi_source.organization: University of Sydney GI Database / Diabetes Care Journal
gi_source.source_match_notes: No direct GI measurement for a triple cheeseburger exists in the University of Sydney GI Database. GI of 66 is taken from the closest analogous entry: hamburger bun-based composite fast-food item (white bread bun dominant carbohydrate matrix, GI ~66 per Atkinson et al. 2008, Diabetes Care 31:2281-2283). Glycemic load calculated as (GI x carbohydrate_g per serving) / 100 = (66 x 79.8) / 100 = 52.7 for a 270 g serving providing approximately 79.8 g carbohydrate.

hypertension.key_factor: High sodium (487 mg/100g) with low potassium-to-sodium ratio (197 mg K vs 487 mg Na per 100g) and saturated fat contributing to arterial stiffness
hypertension.guidance: Individuals with hypertension should avoid or strictly limit triple cheeseburger consumption; a single 270 g serving delivers approximately 1315 mg sodium (57% of the 2300 mg daily limit), and the low potassium offset (532 mg) provides insufficient vascular protection against this sodium load.

type2_diabetes.key_factor: High glycemic load (GL ~53 per serving) driven by refined white-flour bun, combined with saturated fat from triple beef patties and processed cheese worsening insulin resistance
type2_diabetes.guidance: People with Type 2 Diabetes should avoid regular consumption of this item; the refined bun delivers a high glycemic load of approximately 53 per serving, and the saturated fat content from three beef patties and three cheese slices further impairs insulin sensitivity, making blood glucose management significantly more difficult.

cardiovascular_disease.key_factor: Elevated saturated fat (~4.5 g/100g), high sodium (487 mg/100g), cholesterol (29 mg/100g), and probable trans fat from processed beef patties collectively raise LDL cholesterol and cardiovascular risk
cardiovascular_disease.guidance: Individuals with cardiovascular disease should avoid this food; a 270 g serving provides approximately 12 g saturated fat (60% of the 20 g daily limit for a 2000 kcal diet), 1315 mg sodium, and 78 mg cholesterol, all of which contribute to atherogenic dyslipidaemia and elevated blood pressure.

rheumatoid_arthritis.key_factor: High arachidonic acid load from triple beef patties and saturated fat from triple processed cheese slices drive pro-inflammatory eicosanoid production
rheumatoid_arthritis.guidance: People with rheumatoid arthritis should avoid or minimise consumption; the triple beef and triple cheese combination delivers a substantial arachidonic acid and saturated fat burden that promotes systemic inflammation and may exacerbate joint symptoms.

llm_training.natural_language_description: The Triple Cheeseburger is a Western fast-food sandwich consisting of three grilled 100% pure beef patties stacked in a soft sesame-seed hamburger bun, each layer topped with a slice of melted processed cheese. The burger is dressed with tangy dill pickles, diced white onions, tomato ketchup, and yellow mustard. The beef patties are typically seasoned with salt and pepper and cooked on a flat-top grill or broiler, producing a browned exterior with a juicy interior. The processed cheese melts over each patty, contributing a creamy, salty flavour and additional sodium. Per 100 g, the Triple Cheeseburger provides 263 kcal, 13.3 g protein, 10.18 g fat, 29.57 g carbohydrate, 5.95 g sugar, 1.8 g dietary fibre, and 487 mg sodium. A standard serving of approximately 270 g delivers around 710 kcal and 1315 mg sodium. The high sodium content, elevated saturated fat, and high glycemic load (approximately 53 per serving) make this food a high-concern item for individuals managing hypertension, Type 2 Diabetes, or cardiovascular disease. The triple-patty format significantly amplifies the nutritional risks compared with a single-patty cheeseburger. This item is commonly consumed as a lunch or dinner meal in fast-food restaurant settings in Singapore and internationally.
common_user_queries: How many calories in a triple cheeseburger?
Can a diabetic eat a triple cheeseburger?
How much sodium is in a triple cheeseburger?
Is triple cheeseburger suitable for hypertension?

confidence_reason: Core per-100g nutrient values are sourced directly from the HPB Singapore Foodo database entry for Triple Cheeseburger (cr_id G00010121, confidence 0.95, review_status reviewed). Saturated fat is estimated from the closest USDA SR Legacy analogue (double cheeseburger with condiments, USDA 21107) and documented. GI is estimated from the closest analogous fast-food composite entry in the University of Sydney GI Database and Atkinson et al. 2008.
notes: monounsaturated_fat_g set to -1: detailed fatty acid fractionation data for this specific triple cheeseburger composite are not reported in the HPB Foodo database entry for G00010121, and no exact triple-patty cheeseburger match exists in USDA FoodData Central as of 2026; HPB Foodo and USDA FoodData Central SR Legacy were consulted and confirmed unavailability of this fraction for this composite item. polyunsaturated_fat_g set to -1: same basis as monounsaturated_fat_g — fatty acid fractions not reported in HPB Foodo (G00010121) or USDA FoodData Central SR Legacy for this composite as of 2026. trans_fat_g set to -1: trans fat not separately reported in HPB Foodo (G00010121); USDA FoodData Central SR Legacy entry 21107 (closest analogue) does not provide a reliable trans fat figure for this composite preparation as of 2026. vitamin_c_mg set to -1: micronutrient not reported in HPB Foodo (G00010121); USDA FoodData Central SR Legacy 21107 does not provide vitamin C for this composite as of 2026. vitamin_a_mcg_rae set to -1: not reported in HPB Foodo (G00010121) or USDA FoodData Central SR Legacy 21107 for this composite as of 2026. vitamin_b1_thiamine_mg set to -1: not reported in HPB Foodo (G00010121) or USDA FoodData Central SR Legacy 21107 for this composite as of 2026. vitamin_b2_riboflavin_mg set to -1: not reported in HPB Foodo (G00010121) or USDA FoodData Central SR Legacy 21107 for this composite as of 2026. vitamin_b3_niacin_mg set to -1: not reported in HPB Foodo (G00010121) or USDA FoodData Central SR Legacy 21107 for this composite as of 2026. vitamin_b6_mg set to -1: not reported in HPB Foodo (G00010121) or USDA FoodData Central SR Legacy 21107 for this composite as of 2026. vitamin_b12_mcg set to -1: not reported in HPB Foodo (G00010121) or USDA FoodData Central SR Legacy 21107 for this composite as of 2026. folate_mcg_dfe set to -1: not reported in HPB Foodo (G00010121) or USDA FoodData Central SR Legacy 21107 for this composite as of 2026. vitamin_d_mcg set to -1: not reported in HPB Foodo (G00010121) or USDA FoodData Central SR Legacy 21107 for this composite as of 2026. vitamin_e_mg set to -1: not reported in HPB Foodo (G00010121) or USDA FoodData Central SR Legacy 21107 for this composite as of 2026. phosphorus_mg set to -1: not reported in HPB Foodo (G00010121) or USDA FoodData Central SR Legacy 21107 for this composite as of 2026. zinc_mg set to -1: not reported in HPB Foodo (G00010121) or USDA FoodData Central SR Legacy 21107 for this composite as of 2026. selenium_mcg set to -1: not reported in HPB Foodo (G00010121) or USDA FoodData Central SR Legacy 21107 for this composite as of 2026. water_g set to -1: moisture content not reported in HPB Foodo (G00010121) or USDA FoodData Central SR Legacy 21107 for this composite as of 2026. Saturated fat of 4.5 g/100g is adapted from USDA FoodData Central SR Legacy item 21107 (Cheeseburger, double patty, with condiments and vegetables) as the closest approved-source analogue; this is a conservative estimate and may understate the true saturated fat of a triple-patty variant. GI of 66 is an analogous estimate based on the white-flour bun dominant carbohydrate matrix per Atkinson et al. 2008 (Diabetes Care 31:2281-2283) via the University of Sydney GI Database; no direct GI measurement for a triple cheeseburger exists in any approved source. Halal status is not confirmed for this generic record; individual restaurant preparation may vary. This is a Western fast-food item sold in Singapore; no mandatory Singaporean multilingual naming applies as the dish is not a Singaporean-origin dish.
